rows.mentor_reading: This is the baseline VGGT point-cloud quality the mentor asked to compare against.
rows.current_file_evidence: Original predictions path is no longer present locally; numeric evidence is preserved in report.

rows.visual_result: large occupancy gain; not sufficient as final head/face quality
rows.mentor_reading: Crop is useful as an input route because the person occupies more pixels, matching the mentor's recording. It improves retained points, but does not by itself prove clearer 3D face detail.
rows.current_file_evidence: Original predictions path is no longer present locally; report and scene path references remain.

rows.visual_result: occupancy improves, global geometry shifts more than plain crop
rows.mentor_reading: Hard-mask crop is not the best default because it disturbs geometry more than plain human crop.
rows.current_file_evidence: Original predictions path is no longer present locally; numeric evidence is preserved in report.

rows.visual_result: densest ROI, but less stable than plain crop
rows.mentor_reading: Soft matte raises point count most, but the project should not claim quality from point count alone.
rows.current_file_evidence: Original predictions path is no longer present locally; numeric evidence is preserved in report.

rows.visual_result: normal consistency partly improves, but face ROI is below signfix and Open3D remains shell-like
rows.mentor_reading: This directly answers the mentor's normal/depth coupling suggestion: the coupling was implemented and helped some consistency metrics, but did not translate into a modeled face/head point cloud.
rows.current_file_evidence: Original predictions path is no longer present locally; preview directory is empty after cleanup.

rows.family: VGGT + crop + weak SMPL-X prior + self-geometry
rows.visual_result: negative strict visual review; shell/ghost head-face and fragmented hands
rows.mentor_reading: Combining crop and self-geometry was a reasonable VGGT line, but it still failed the visual gate.
rows.current_file_evidence: Local inference directories remain, but no predictions.npz is present in this checkout.

rows.full_points: mean valid 11575.5 per view in V50R2 comparison
rows.visual_result: basic full-body point-map output exists; no normal route
rows.mentor_reading: This is the current same-protocol base VGGT comparison used for V50R2.

rows.world_l2_vs_full: mean L2 vs V25 all pixels = 0.00053544
rows.visual_result: normal evidence available; main point-map coordinate change from V25 is small
rows.mentor_reading: This is the true prior-enabled VGGT path; the honest conclusion is that point-map coordinates changed little.

rows.family: VGGT candidate package with SMPL-X native evidence
rows.full_points: same main point map as V42
rows.head_points: tighter packaged head/face evidence, mean valid 2450.83 per view in comparison
rows.face_points: packaged region evidence, not a larger raw point count
rows.world_l2_vs_full: V50R2 candidate points vs V42 max abs = 0.0
rows.visual_result: formal candidate closure and region evidence; not a new visibly sharper full-body point field
rows.mentor_reading: V50R2 is defensible as a candidate package, but detail improvement over base VGGT must be described cautiously.
